sanskrit: तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः । कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥
hindiTranslation: सकामभाववाले तपस्वियोंसे भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है, ज्ञानियोंसे भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है और कर्मियोंसे भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है  ऐसा मेरा मत है ।  अतः हे अर्जुन  तू योगी हो जा ॥
englishTranslation: The yogi is greater than ascetics, greater than scholars, and greater than workers. Therefore, Arjuna, become a yogi.
essence: Inner mastery outranks every outer path.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Meditation and inner mastery are higher than harsh austerity, intellectual learning, or mere action. Krishna urges Arjuna to become a true yogi.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing that inner steadiness matters more than any single method or achievement.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna is closing his teaching on meditation. After showing that practice can survive failure and carry across lives, he now ranks the yogi above austerity, knowledge, and action, then directly tells Arjuna to become one.
modernRelevance: You can meditate for years, read every book, and still feel split when the hard choice arrives at midnight. The real test is whether your mind can stay centered when action is unavoidable.
translationLiteral: तपस्विभ्योऽधिकः (greater than ascetics) / योगी (the yogi) / ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि (even than the wise) / मतोऽधिकः (is considered greater) / कर्मिभ्यः (than workers) / च (and) / अधिकः (greater) / योगी (the yogi) / तस्मात् (therefore) / योगी (a yogi) / भव (become) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
insight: It shifts the goal from collecting spiritual methods to becoming inwardly integrated.

quoteVariants.text: The yogi stands above austerity, learning, and work. Become that one, Arjuna.
Be the yogi, not the worker alone.
Effort matters, but the yogi stands above it.
Become the yogi above all methods.
